apply: Use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions involving sine, cosine, and tangent.
Apply trigonometric functions to solve problems involving right triangles.
Use trigonometric functions to solve problems involving periodic phenomena, such as sound and light waves.
analyze: Analyze the relationships between the sine, cosine, and tangent functions and their graphs.
Examine the impact of the amplitude and period on the graph of a trigonometric function.
Compare and contrast different trigonometric functions and their properties.
remember: Recall the definitions of trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, tangent) for different angles.
State the range of the six trigonometric functions.
Identify the quadrant in which a given angle lies based on its reference angle.
understand: Explain the relationship between trigonometric functions and their reciprocal functions (cosecant, secant, cotangent).
Describe the periodic nature of trigonometric functions and their graphs.
Understand the concept of principal value and the range of principal value.

ncert_objectives: Students will be able to define the six trigonometric functions and their domains and ranges.
Students will be able to explain the periodic nature of trigonometric functions and their graphs.
Students will be able to use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions involving sine, cosine, and tangent.
board_exam_objectives: Use trigonometric functions to solve problems involving right triangles (e.g., finding missing sides or angles).
Apply trigonometric functions to solve problems involving periodic phenomena (e.g., sound and light waves).
Use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions involving sine, cosine, and tangent.
